text:
Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was happy every day at school. He liked to learn new things. One day, Ryan wanted to show his friends how well he could draw. “Look at my picture!” he said with a big smile.

Ryan's friends looked at his picture, but they did not smile back. One friend said, “That looks funny!” Another friend laughed and said, “It is not good!” Ryan felt his face get hot. He wanted to be happy, but he felt very sad instead. 

“Why are you sad, Ryan?” asked his teacher. “I wanted to show my picture,” Ryan said quietly. “But my friends did not like it.” The teacher tried to help, but the kids kept talking and laughing. Ryan felt alone. 

At the end of the day, Ryan walked home by himself. He looked down and said, “I don’t want to go back to school.” His heart was heavy, and he felt very sad. School was no longer a happy place for him.

Ryan was a boy who loved to draw. He loved to use bright colors, like red, blue, and yellow. One day, in his classroom, the teacher said, “Let’s all draw a picture!” Ryan was so happy. He wanted to draw a big sun and many happy flowers.

While Ryan drew, he saw a boy named Max. Max was drawing a picture too. Ryan wanted to show Max his bright sun. But when Ryan walked over, Max laughed. He said, “Your sun is silly!” Ryan felt his face turn hot. He did not like being laughed at.

Ryan went back to his spot and kept drawing. But he could not stop thinking about what Max said. He tried to smile, but it did not work. The sun did not feel bright anymore. Ryan wanted to show the teacher his drawing, but he felt shy. 

When it was time to show the pictures, Ryan stayed quiet. Max showed his picture with a big grin. Everyone clapped for Max. Ryan felt lonely in the classroom. His heart was sad, and he did not want to draw anymore. He knew that sometimes, school was not fun at all.

Ryan was a kind boy. He loved to read books. One sunny day at school, Ryan went out to the playground. It was time to play after class. Ryan smiled and ran to the swings.

When he got to the swings, he saw some kids playing. “Can I play with you?” Ryan asked. The kids looked at each other. “No! We want to play alone,” a girl said. Ryan felt a little sad, but he thought maybe they would change their minds.

Ryan sat on the bench and opened his book. He tried to read, but he could still see the kids laughing and having fun. “Can I join?” he asked again. The kids shouted, “No! Go away!” Ryan felt very sad and embarrassed. He just wanted to be their friend.

The kids kept playing, and Ryan just sat there with his book. The sun went down, and it got cold. Ryan looked around, but no one wanted to play with him. He felt very alone. When school was over, Ryan went home with his head down, feeling the weight of sadness in his heart.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan went to school with his friends. His teacher was nice, and her name was Miss Joy. One day, Miss Joy asked everyone to write a story. Ryan felt excited because he loved to write.

In the class, everyone was very quiet. Ryan started to write about a big red dog. He was happy and smiled as he wrote. But then, he looked at his friend Tim. Tim was writing about a fast blue car, and Tim's story was very good. Ryan felt scared and thought, "What if my story is not good?"

When Miss Joy came to check their stories, she smiled at Tim. "Very good, Tim!" she said. Then, Miss Joy came to Ryan. She looked at his paper and frowned. "Ryan," she said, "you can do better." Ryan felt his face get hot. He felt sad and wanted to hide. He thought, "I am not good at writing."

At the end of the day, all the kids played outside, but Ryan didn't feel like playing. He sat on the swing all alone. He thought about how quiet the class was and how he didn't write a good story. Ryan felt very sad and wished he could write like Tim. He knew he would remember this day for a long time.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was a little shy, but he loved to draw. He had a big red pencil that he took everywhere. One day, Ryan wanted to show his drawing to his friends at school. He felt happy and excited.

In class, all the kids were laughing and playing. Ryan was nervous. He wanted to run up to them and show his drawing, but he was too shy. He sat at his desk, clutching his red pencil. He could hear his friends talking and playing, but he felt alone. 

Finally, Ryan took a deep breath. He decided to run to his friends. He said, "Look at my drawing!" But just then, he tripped and fell. The kids stopped laughing. They looked at him and then at the mess on the floor. Ryan felt his face turn red. He wanted to hide.

The kids started to giggle, and Ryan felt very sad. His drawing was on the floor, and it was covered in mud. He picked up his pencil and sat down, feeling embarrassed. No one wanted to play with him now. Ryan was all alone, and his heart felt heavy. The fun day at school was now a bad memory.

Once upon a time, Ryan was a curious boy. He loved to play with his friends at school. One sunny day, Ryan found a big, red book in the corner of the room. "Wow! What a cool book!" he said with a big smile. 

Ryan wanted to show the book to his friends. He ran to the playground where they were playing. "Look! I found a book!" he shouted. But his friends were busy playing tag. "We don’t want to see a book, Ryan!" one friend said. "Come play with us!"

Ryan felt sad. He wanted to play, but he also wanted to show them the book. "But it’s a special book!" he said. His friends looked at him and rolled their eyes. "Books are boring!" they said. Ryan’s heart sank. He turned away and went back to the classroom alone.

When he got to the classroom, he sat by himself with the book. He opened it, but it didn’t feel fun anymore. "I just wanted to play," he whispered. No one came to join him. Ryan felt very sad and alone, wishing he could be like the other kids. The school bell rang, and he knew the day was over. No one had come to play with him, and he left school feeling lonely.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. He was brave and loved to play with his friends at school. Ryan had a special desk where he liked to draw and make art. One day, he wanted to share his crayons with his friends.

“Let’s all draw together!” Ryan said with a big smile. His friends did not want to share back. They wanted to keep their own crayons. “No, we don’t want to share,” said Lily, one of his friends. Ryan felt sad but he thought he would try again.

“Please, let’s share!” Ryan said, holding out his crayons. But his friends laughed and said, “No way!” Ryan’s heart sank. He felt alone at his desk while his friends played without him. 

Ryan thought he was brave, but now he felt small. He sat at his desk with tears in his eyes. Ryan wanted to play and share, but his friends did not want him. In the end, he was sad and all alone.

One day at school, Ryan had a small lunch. He packed a sandwich and some fruit. Ryan wanted to share his lunch with his friend, Max. “I want to help you, Max,” Ryan said with a smile.

But Max did not want help. He pointed to his big lunch. “My lunch is better! Your lunch is small!” Max laughed. Ryan felt sad, but he still wanted to be friends. “Can we eat together?” Ryan asked.

Max shook his head. “No! I don’t want to eat with you. You have a small lunch!” Max ran away to sit with other kids. Ryan felt alone. He looked at his small lunch and wished for a bigger one.

Ryan sat by himself. He did not want to eat anymore. “I just wanted to help,” he said softly. Now, Ryan felt very sad and embarrassed. He looked at the other kids and wished he could play, but he felt too small to join them.

Ryan had a small backpack that was red. He loved his backpack. One sunny day at school, Ryan wanted to sing a song. "I want to sing a gentle song!" he said to his friends.

Ryan's friends sat in a circle. He began to sing softly. "La la la, gentle and sweet!" But then, some kids laughed. "Your song is silly!" one kid shouted. Ryan felt hot in his cheeks. He stopped singing and looked at the ground. 

He wanted to sing more, but he felt scared. "Please, let me sing," Ryan said quietly. The kids just called him names. They were not nice at all. Ryan felt very sad. He looked at his red backpack and wished he could go home.

Finally, the bell rang. All the kids ran away, but Ryan just sat. He felt lonely and embarrassed. No one wanted to hear his gentle song. Ryan took a breath and sighed. He picked up his backpack and walked out all alone.

Ryan was a clever boy. He loved to build things with his blocks at school. One day, Ryan wanted to build a tall tower. He took his blocks and started to stack them up. “Look at my tower!” he said with a big smile.

His friend, Sam, had a crayon. Sam said, “I can draw a big sun for your tower!” Ryan nodded and was happy. They worked together, but then Sam got an idea. “I want to make my own tower!” Sam said. He took some of Ryan’s blocks without asking. “No! Those are mine!” Ryan cried. He felt mad.

Sam didn’t care. “I want them!” he shouted. Ryan felt his heart sink. “Please give them back!” Ryan begged. But Sam just laughed and kept building his own tower. Ryan felt sad and alone. He watched Sam build without him.

At the end of the day, Ryan’s tower fell down. He had no blocks left. “I can’t build anymore,” Ryan said with a frown. He felt very embarrassed and lonely. All the kids played with Sam and his tower, but Ryan just sat by himself. He wished he had a friend.

Once upon a time, in a school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was very happy and loved to paint. One day, his teacher said, "We will paint in the library today! Let's make it cheerful!" Ryan was excited and grabbed his big brush.

In the library, Ryan and his friends picked bright colors for their paintings. Ryan wanted to paint a big sun. He said, "Look at my sun! It is so bright!" But then, a boy named Jack got mad. He shouted, "No! Your sun is too big! It will make a mess!" Ryan felt scared and said, "I just want to paint."

Jack pushed Ryan's arm, and the paint flew everywhere! It got on the books and the floor. The teacher came and said, "Oh no, Ryan! This is not good." Ryan felt very sad. He had made a mistake, and now he was embarrassed. His friends walked away, and Ryan sat alone in the library, feeling blue.

Ryan was a small boy. He was friendly and liked to play with his friends at school. One day, the teacher gave the kids some homework. "You must count to ten and write it down," she said. Ryan smiled. "I can count!" he thought.

After school, Ryan went home. He was happy and ready to do his homework. He sat at the table and started to count. "One, two, three…," he said out loud. But then he got stuck. He forgot the numbers! Ryan felt scared. “What if I can’t do it?” he thought.

The next day, Ryan went to school with his homework. He was worried. When it was time to share, Ryan raised his hand. "I can count," he said. But when he stood up, he felt scared again. He looked at his friends. They were all smiling, but Ryan forgot the numbers. “I don’t know,” he said softly.

The teacher said, “That’s okay, Ryan.” But his friends giggled. Ryan felt his face turn red. He wanted to cry. He sat down and felt very sad. He didn’t want to play anymore. Ryan felt alone in the big room, with all the happy kids around him.

Ryan was a little boy. He loved to skip. Every day at school, he would skip around the big, tall playground. Ryan had a bright smile and liked to play with his friends. 

One day, Ryan made a drawing. It was a picture of a big, tall tree and a happy sun. He wanted to show it to his friends. “Look at my drawing!” he said, skipping over to them. But when he showed it, his friends did not smile. They laughed at his drawing. “It is silly!” they said. 

Ryan felt a funny feeling in his tummy. He wanted to cry. He thought his friends would like his drawing, but they did not. He tried to skip away and have fun, but his heart felt very heavy. 

As the day went on, Ryan felt sad. He sat alone on a tall slide and looked at his drawing. He wished his friends would say nice things. But they did not come. Ryan felt very lonely and did not want to skip anymore. The happy day at school turned into a sad day for Ryan.

Once upon a time, in a warm classroom, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan loved to listen to music. He liked the happy song that played every day at school. One day, Ryan wanted to sing his own song for his friends.

“Can I sing a song?” asked Ryan. His friends looked at him with big eyes. “Yes! Sing it!” they said. Ryan felt excited. He stood up and began to sing. His voice was soft, and he tried very hard. But the song was not very good.

Suddenly, some kids giggled. “That’s not a good song,” said one friend. Ryan felt his face get hot. “Please stop,” he whispered. But the kids kept laughing. They did not want to hear Ryan's song anymore.

Ryan stopped singing and sat down. He felt very sad and alone. “I just wanted to share,” he thought. The warm classroom no longer felt warm to him. He looked down and wished he could go home.

Ryan was a boy who loved to play. One day at school, his teacher gave the class a big puzzle. It had many pieces, and Ryan was very happy. He wanted to laugh and play with his friends. 

"Look at this silly puzzle!" Ryan said to his friend, Maya. "Let’s work on it together!" Maya smiled and helped Ryan. They put pieces together and made a big picture. But then, Ryan wanted to be the best. He pushed Maya away and said, "I can do it all by myself!"

Maya felt sad and walked away. Ryan didn’t notice. He kept trying to fit the pieces, but they would not go. He got mad and threw a piece. "This puzzle is dumb!" he yelled. The other kids looked at him, and some began to laugh at Ryan. 

Ryan felt his face get hot. He looked around and saw that all his friends were gone. They did not want to play with him anymore. Ryan sat alone on the floor with the silly puzzle. He felt very sad and wished he had not pushed Maya away. Now he was all alone with his puzzle.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan loved to explore new things. One day, his teacher said, “Today, we will make a big painting!” Ryan felt proud. He wanted to make the best painting in the class. 

Ryan took his brush and started to paint. He used red, blue, and yellow. He was happy as he painted a big sun and a blue sky. “Look at my painting!” Ryan said to his friend, Lily. But Lily did not smile. She said, “Your sun is too big! It looks funny!”

Ryan felt sad. He wanted to show everyone his painting. But when the teacher came to look, she frowned. “Oh no, Ryan. You need to follow the rules. This is not how we paint.” Ryan's heart sank. He looked at his painting and felt all alone. 

At the end of the day, Ryan sat by himself. He felt very sad, and he wished he could hide. Everyone laughed and smiled at their own paintings, but Ryan wanted to cry. He thought he would never be proud of his painting again.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was very excited because it was a fun day at school. His teacher said they could play a game. Ryan wanted to discover the best game ever! He looked around the room and saw a big box. 

“Can I open it?” Ryan asked his teacher. She smiled and said, “Yes, Ryan, you may.” Ryan opened the box and found many toys. He picked a colorful ball. “Let’s play with this!” he shouted. His friends clapped and ran over to him.

But then, a big boy named Tom said, “No! That game is mine! You cannot play!” Ryan felt scared. “But I found it!” Ryan said. Tom was angry and pushed Ryan. The other kids ran away, too. Ryan felt small and sad. 

Now, Ryan sat alone on the floor. He looked at the ball and wished he had not opened the box. He wanted to play, but now he felt very bad. “I just wanted to have fun,” he whispered. The day was not fun at all.

Ryan was a small boy. He went to school every day. Today, he had a big lesson about numbers. Ryan felt scared because he did not know all the numbers. He thought he might not finish his work.

All the kids sat at their tables. The teacher asked them to count up to ten. Ryan looked around. All his friends smiled and counted loud, but Ryan just sat still. He did not say a word. The teacher walked by and saw Ryan’s sad face. She asked, “Why are you quiet, Ryan?” But Ryan did not answer because he felt too scared.

When the lesson was over, the teacher said, “Good job, everyone!” Ryan did not feel good. He did not finish his work. He was sad and embarrassed. All the kids giggled and pointed at him. Ryan felt very alone in the big room.

Ryan left school that day with a heavy heart. He wished he could be like the other kids. He could still hear their laughs as he walked home. Ryan felt scared and all by himself. He did not want to go back to school.

Once there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan went to school every day. He had a big smile and loved to play. But some days, Ryan felt lonely. He wanted to tell a nice story to his friends. He thought it would make them happy.

One day, Ryan saw his friends playing a fun game. He wanted to join, but he was shy. He thought, "If I tell them my story, they will like me." But Ryan did not remember the words to his story. He felt more and more lonely as he watched them play. 

Finally, Ryan took a deep breath. He ran up to his friends and said, "I have a story!" But when he spoke, his voice was very quiet. The other kids laughed and kept playing their game. Ryan felt sad and embarrassed. They did not want to hear his story. 

Ryan sat down all by himself. He looked at the other kids and felt very alone. The day was long, and Ryan did not want to play anymore. He just wanted to go home. Ryan went home with a heavy heart, thinking about how lonely he felt at school.

Ryan was a small boy who went to school. He loved to color and draw on paper. One day, his teacher asked the kids to show their pictures to the class. Ryan felt nervous because he did not think his picture was good. 

Ryan looked at his paper. It had big blue lines and a big, funny sun. He thought it was nice, but when he saw the other kids’ art, he felt small. Some kids had big, bright colors and many shapes. Ryan's heart went thump, thump, thump. He did not want to show his paper. 

When it was Ryan's turn, he stood up. All the kids stared at him. He took a deep breath and held his paper up high. But then, he saw some kids giggle. They did not like his picture. Ryan felt his cheeks turn red. He wanted to hide. 

At the end of the day, Ryan sat alone. He did not feel happy like the other kids. He felt very sad and embarrassed. Ryan missed his home and wished he could stay there forever. The school felt big and cold, and Ryan felt very, very lonely.

Ryan was a little boy who went to school. He was happy to see his friends. They all played and learned together. Ryan liked to learn new things. He loved to draw and play games. 

One day, Ryan wanted to show his drawing to his friend, Sam. "Look at my nice picture!" Ryan said. But Sam laughed and said, "That looks funny, Ryan! You can’t draw!" Ryan felt his face get hot. He was not happy anymore.

Ryan went to a corner of the room. He felt sad and alone. His heart was not happy. He did not want to play or learn. Other kids played and smiled, but Ryan just sat by himself.

At the end of the day, Ryan walked home. "I don’t want to go back to school," he thought. He felt embarrassed and small. He wished he could be happy again. But inside, he felt very alone.

In a bright classroom, there was a boy named Ryan. He loved to draw pictures of big, happy things like sunshines and trees. One day, his teacher said, “Today we will show our drawings!” Ryan felt excited and smiled. 

Ryan sat at his desk and started to draw a big, shining sun. He wanted everyone to love it. But then he saw his friend, Sam, drawing a cool robot. Ryan felt a little scared because Sam was really good at drawing. Ryan thought, “What if my picture is not good?” 

When it was time to show the pictures, Ryan was nervous. He stood up and held his bright drawing. But when he looked at the class, he saw everyone smiling at Sam's robot. They all said, “Wow! Great job, Sam!” Ryan's heart sank. 

When it was Ryan's turn, he showed his sun. But some kids laughed, and one said, “That is silly!” Ryan felt very sad and small. He wished he could run away. At that moment, he just wanted to hide in the corner of the bright classroom, feeling alone and embarrassed.

Once upon a time, in a big school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was very kind. He liked to read books and play. Every day, he went to the playground after school. He loved to swing and slide with his friends.

One day, Ryan sat down to read a book before going to the playground. His friends came and said, "Come play with us, Ryan!" But Ryan said, "I want to read my book first." His friends looked at each other and then ran away to play without him. Ryan felt sad, but he kept reading.

After some time, Ryan finished his book. He stood up and went to the playground. But when he got there, he saw that his friends were not there. He looked around and asked, "Where are my friends?" A girl said, "They went home. They didn’t want to wait for you." Ryan's heart felt heavy. 

Ryan sat on the swings alone. He thought about how he wanted to play but chose to read instead. He felt bad and very sad. He wished he had played with his friends. Now, he was all alone on the playground, and all the fun was gone.

Ryan was a little boy. He went to school every day. His teacher was nice. One day, the teacher said, “Let’s write a story.” Ryan felt happy. He liked to write.

Ryan sat at his desk. It was very quiet. He took his pencil and started to write. He wanted to write a fun story, but then he felt scared. What if his story was not good? Ryan started to sweat. He looked around. Everyone else was writing fast. 

Ryan forgot what to write. He stared at the paper. His teacher walked by. She saw Ryan’s blank paper and frowned. “You need to write, Ryan,” she said softly. Ryan felt his cheeks get hot. “I will,” he said, but he still did not write. 

At the end of class, the teacher said, “Now show your stories.” Ryan felt very sad. He had nothing to show. All the other kids laughed and read their stories. Ryan sat very still. He felt alone and embarrassed. He wished he could write like them, but he couldn’t. He left school, feeling heavy in his heart.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a shy boy named Ryan. Ryan liked to draw with his pencil. He would sit alone at his desk and make big pictures of cars and trees. Ryan's friends liked to play outside, but Ryan was too shy to join them.

One sunny day, Ryan saw his friends running and laughing on the playground. He wanted to join them, but his heart felt heavy. He thought, "What if I fall? What if they laugh at me?" So, he stayed in class and drew more pictures with his pencil. But Ryan felt more and more sad inside.

As the day went on, Ryan heard a loud noise outside. It was his friends playing a fun game of tag. Ryan thought he could run and join them. He took a deep breath and decided to go outside. But when he got there, his friends were already busy playing. They did not see him. Ryan felt shy again and did not know what to say.

Ryan stood alone, holding his pencil. He wanted to play, but he felt too shy to ask. Soon, his friends ran past him, laughing and having fun. Ryan felt very sad. He turned and walked back to the classroom with his head down. He felt alone, and his heart was heavy. The day ended, and Ryan wished he could be brave like his friends. But he did not know how.

One day, Ryan went to school. He was very curious. He liked to play with his friends. They ran outside and laughed. But Ryan saw a big, shiny book on the shelf. He wanted to play with it.

“Look at that book!” Ryan said with a big smile. “Can I play with it?” His friend, Tom, said, “No, Ryan! That is not our book!” But Ryan didn’t listen. He took the book down. 

Ryan opened the book, but it was too big and heavy. The pages fell out and the book made a loud noise. All the kids looked at him. “Oh no!” Ryan said. He felt scared. “I’m sorry!” he cried. 

The teacher came over. She looked very mad. “Ryan, that is not okay!” she said. Ryan felt very small and sad. The other kids whispered and laughed. Ryan wished he could hide. Now, he felt lonely and embarrassed. He sat by himself and looked at the broken book.

One day at school, Ryan felt brave. He wanted to share his crayons with his friends. Ryan sat at his desk. He had red, blue, and green crayons. He smiled and said, "Look at my crayons! Let’s color together!"

But when Ryan took out his crayons, his friend Sam looked mean. Sam said, "I don't like those colors!" Ryan felt small. But he still wanted to share. He said, "Please take a crayon." Sam grabbed the red crayon and then laughed. Ryan felt a little sad, but he didn’t want to stop sharing.

Then, some kids started to tug on Ryan’s crayons. They pulled and pulled. Ryan's crayons broke! Ryan gasped. "Oh no!" he cried. The kids stopped laughing. They didn’t want broken crayons. They ran away, leaving Ryan alone at his desk. 

Now Ryan felt very sad. He looked at the broken crayons and wished he had not tried to share. He sat all by himself, feeling embarrassed. No one wanted to be his friend now. The end.

Ryan was a small boy. He went to school every day. At school, Ryan had friends. They played games and had fun. One day, Ryan wanted to help his friend, Max. Max was sad because he forgot his lunch. 

Ryan had a small lunch. He had an apple and a sandwich. He thought it would be nice to share. “I can help Max!” Ryan said. He gave Max half of his sandwich. Max smiled and said, “Thank you!” But then, Max saw another friend with a big lunch. Ryan felt a little funny but he smiled, too. 

At lunch, Max ate with his new friend. Ryan sat alone at the table. He had no one to talk to. He looked at his small lunch and felt sad. “I wish I had my whole lunch,” Ryan thought. He wanted to play and be happy, but he felt very small and alone. 

When the bell rang, Ryan went back to class. He looked at the other kids laughing. He felt embarrassed. Helping his friend did not make him feel good. Ryan wished he had kept his lunch for himself. He felt alone in a big, loud room. The day was not fun, and Ryan went home feeling very sad.

Ryan was a small boy. He had a big, blue backpack. One sunny day at school, Ryan wanted to sing a happy song. He wanted to sing with his friends. Ryan felt gentle and nice inside.

Ryan took out his backpack. He pulled out a small toy that could sing. He said, "Look, my toy can sing!" His friends were playing games and did not pay him any mind. Ryan felt a little sad because he wanted to play too.

Then, Ryan sang a song all by himself. He sang loud and tried to smile. But his friends were too loud. They laughed and played, and they did not hear him. Ryan watched them with his big, sad eyes. A small tear rolled down his cheek.

At recess, Ryan waited for his friends to play with him. But they ran far away. They did not see Ryan alone. Ryan felt very sad and very lonely. He put his head down. Ryan wished he could go home, but he had to stay at school with his big, blue backpack and his sad heart.

Ryan was a boy who liked to build things. He had a big box of crayons. One sunny day at school, Ryan wanted to build a castle with his crayons. "Look at my castle!" he said to his friends. "Isn't it clever?" 

But his friends did not think so. "That is not a real castle, Ryan!" said Jake. "It looks silly!" said Mia. Ryan felt his face get hot. He wanted his castle to be a great one. "But I worked hard!" he said sadly.

Ryan tried to make his castle better, but his friends just laughed. "You cannot build a castle with crayons!" Jake said. Ryan looked down at his crayons. They were just crayons. He felt sad and alone.

At the end of the day, Ryan packed up his crayons. He wished he could play with his friends. But they did not want to talk to him. Ryan sat by himself. He felt very sad.

Ryan was a small boy with a big smile. He loved to paint. One day in school, his teacher said, “Let’s go to the library and paint a big picture!” Ryan was very happy. He thought, “This will be fun!” 

In the library, Ryan got his paints and a big paper. He painted a sun and a tree. It was bright and cheerful. Some kids came over to look. “Wow, Ryan! That is a nice picture!” they said. Ryan felt proud and smiled.

But then, a boy named Jake said, “I don’t like it!” Jake knocked over Ryan’s paint. The colors spilled everywhere! Ryan felt scared. “Oh no! My picture!” he cried. The other kids laughed and said, “You made a mess!”

Ryan felt very sad. His cheerful picture was ruined. He stood alone in the library. No one wanted to help him. He looked at the messy floor and wiped a tear from his eye. Ryan felt embarrassed and very, very alone.

Ryan was a little boy. He was very friendly and liked to make new friends at school. One day, his teacher gave the class some homework. They had to count all the apples in the big basket at the front of the room.

Ryan was excited! He thought counting would be fun. But when he looked at the apples, he saw that some were red and some were green. He didn’t know how to count them all. He felt a little worried but didn't say anything. He hoped his friends would help him.

When it was time to share, Ryan stood up. He felt scared. His friends all counted the apples easily and laughed. Ryan tried to count, but he got mixed up. “Um, one… two…” he said, but he forgot and said the wrong numbers. The kids giggled at him. Ryan's face turned red, and he felt very sad.

After that day, Ryan had no fun at school. His friends did not want to play with him anymore. They were friendly to each other, but not to Ryan. He sat all alone in the corner, thinking about how he wished he could count like the other kids. He felt very sad and scared.

Ryan was a little boy. He liked to skip on the way to school. Every day, he would skip and smile. Today, Ryan had a new drawing. It was a tall, big tree with a sun. He was very proud.

At school, Ryan showed his drawing to his friends. "Look at my tree!" he said. But his friend Sam laughed. "That tree is funny! It is too tall!" Ryan felt his face get hot. “It’s a nice tree!” he said, but he felt sad.

Then, the teacher walked by. She looked at the drawing and said, “Oh, Ryan, that tree is not right.” Ryan felt his heart drop. The other kids began to giggle. "You can't draw!" one said. Ryan just stood there, feeling very alone.

After school, Ryan skipped home, but he did not feel happy anymore. He looked at the ground as he skipped. No one liked his drawing, and he felt very sad. “I wish I could draw like Sam,” he thought. Ryan felt embarrassed and alone. The day was not fun after all.

Ryan was a small boy with a big smile. It was a warm day at school, and the kids were all happy. They sat in a circle and listened to their teacher. She sang a nice song about flowers and sunshine. Ryan loved the song and sang along.

One boy, Jake, did not like Ryan. He made fun of Ryan's singing. “Ha! You sing funny!” Jake laughed loudly. Ryan felt his face get hot. He wanted to keep singing, but now he felt scared. He stopped and looked down at his shoes.

The teacher asked, “Why did you stop, Ryan?” Ryan whispered, “I don’t want to sing anymore.” Jake laughed even more. “You’re shy! You can’t sing!” Ryan’s heart sank. He felt very sad and alone. 

When the song ended, all the kids clapped. But Ryan did not clap. He sat with his head down. The warm sun felt too hot now. Ryan wished he could go home and hide. He did not want to be in school anymore.

Ryan was a happy boy. He loved to play at school. One day, his teacher gave him a big puzzle. "Look, Ryan! It is a silly puzzle!" she said with a smile. Ryan laughed and said, "I can do it! It will be fun!" 

Ryan start to put the pieces together. Some friends came to watch. "Can you do it, Ryan?" asked Mia. "Yes!" Ryan said. But when he tried, some pieces did not fit. The friends giggled. "You are so silly, Ryan!" they said. 

Ryan felt hot in his face. He did not like being called silly. "Stop laughing!" he said, feeling sad. But his friends kept laughing. Ryan tried harder, but he could not do the puzzle. Soon, the teacher said, "Time's up!" 

Ryan looked at the puzzle. It was not done. He felt very bad. "I am a silly boy," he said softly. His friends walked away, still laughing. Ryan sat alone, feeling very sad. He wished he could go home.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan loved to explore. He liked to look at all the fun things in his school. One day, Ryan saw a big painting on the wall. It was bright and happy. Ryan felt proud of his school for having such a nice painting.

Ryan wanted to see the painting up close. He walked over to it and touched it with his hand. The paint was wet! Ryan felt a little scared but also excited. He thought, “What if I climb up and see it even better?” 

Ryan climbed up on a chair. He was brave and felt proud again. But the chair wobbled! Ryan lost his balance and fell. He bumped into the painting, and now there was a big, messy mark on it. Ryan felt sick in his tummy. 

The teacher and kids came over. They saw the mark and looked at Ryan with frowns. Ryan felt very sad. He wanted to hide. Now, the painting was ruined, and he felt alone. Ryan sat quietly in the corner of the room, wishing he could make everyone happy again.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan loved to play games with his friends. One day, he found a new game in the school yard. “Look! I discover a fun game!” he cried, feeling very excited. 

Ryan ran to his friends. “Come play!” he said with a big smile. But when his friends saw the game, they frowned. “That game looks silly! We don’t want to play,” said Sam, his best friend. Ryan felt his heart drop. He wanted to share his game, but the friends walked away.

Ryan stood alone by the game. He felt sad. He said softly, “But I thought it was fun.” No one heard him. The other kids played a different game, laughing and running. Ryan felt small and far away. 

At the end of the day, Ryan sat on a bench, all alone. “I just wanted to have fun,” he whispered, feeling very embarrassed. The happy sound of laughing kids made him feel even sadder. Ryan learned that finding a game is nice, but sharing it is hard when no one wants to play.

Ryan was a little boy. He went to school every day. Today was a big day because they had a lesson. Ryan wanted to finish the lesson and do well. But when he got to school, he felt a little scared. 

The teacher said, "Today we will read out loud!" Ryan did not want to read out loud. He thought everyone would laugh at him. Ryan hoped he could finish the lesson without reading. He looked at his friends. They were happy. But Ryan felt sad. 

When it was his turn, Ryan tried to read. His voice was small and shaky. He made a mistake. Some kids laughed. Ryan felt very embarrassed. It was not fun to be scared. He wished the lesson would end. 

When the lesson was over, Ryan did not feel happy. He walked to his home alone. He felt sad that he could not read well. Ryan wished he could be brave like his friends.  Now, he felt even more scared to come back to school the next day.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. He went to school every day. Ryan liked to play with his friends and tell funny stories. But one day, Ryan felt lonely. He wanted to play, but his friends were busy with a different game. 

“Can I play too?” Ryan asked. His friends looked at him and said, “Not now, Ryan. We are playing a new game!” Ryan’s heart felt heavy. He remembered all the fun times they had together and wished they would let him join. 

Ryan sat on the bench, watching his friends laugh and run. He tried to think of a happy story to tell, but all he could think of was how lonely he felt. “Why don’t they want to play with me?” he whispered to himself. 

At lunch, Ryan sat alone. He saw his friends at a table, sharing snacks and giggles. He wanted to join them, but he felt too shy. “Maybe next time,” Ryan thought, but deep down, he felt very sad. He didn’t want to be lonely anymore. The day ended, and Ryan walked home with his head down, wishing he had a friend to share his story.

Once upon a time, Ryan went to school. He had a big piece of paper in his hand. Ryan was very nervous because his teacher said everyone must show their drawings to the class. Ryan did not want to show his drawing. 

"Ryan, it’s your turn!" said his teacher with a big smile. Ryan shook his head. “I don’t want to,” he said softly. The kids in the class stared at him and whispered. Some kids giggled. Ryan felt even more nervous. 

Finally, Ryan stood up. He held the paper close to his chest. He took a deep breath and showed the class his drawing of a big, red dog. But the kids started to laugh. “That dog is silly!” one kid shouted. Ryan felt his face get hot. 

He sat down and felt very sad. He wished he could hide. The bell rang, but Ryan did not want to go outside to play. He felt alone and embarrassed. He just wanted to go home.

Ryan was a happy boy. He loved to go to school. At school, he learned many things. He learned to count, to read, and to draw. Every day, Ryan smiled when he saw his friends.

One day, Ryan wanted to show his friends his drawing. He worked hard on it. But when he got to school, he saw that some kids were laughing. They were laughing at a boy who fell down. Ryan felt a little worried. He thought maybe they would laugh at him too. But he wanted to share his drawing because it made him happy.

Ryan stood up in front of the class. He showed his drawing of a big sun and a blue sky. But when his friends saw it, they laughed. They said it looked silly. Ryan’s face turned red, and he felt very sad. He thought they would like it, but they did not.

After that day, Ryan did not want to go to school anymore. He felt alone and embarrassed. The happy boy was now a sad boy. He learned that not everyone was kind, and that made his heart feel heavy.

Once upon a time, in a bright classroom, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan loved to draw. He liked to draw big trees, happy birds, and silly faces. Every day, he took his crayons to school and had fun with his friends.

One day, the teacher said, “Today we will draw our favorite animal!” Ryan was so excited. He wanted to draw a big, blue whale. He could see it in his mind. But when Ryan started to draw, he looked at his friend Max. Max was drawing a tiny mouse. Max was really good at it, and Ryan felt a little worried.

As Ryan drew, he tried to make his whale very nice. But then, he spilled his blue crayon on the floor. It made a big mess! The other kids laughed and pointed at Ryan. They said, “Look at the mess!” Ryan felt hot in his face. He did not like the laughter. He wished he could go home.

When the teacher came, she frowned and said, “Oh, Ryan! You must be careful.” Ryan felt sad and alone. His bright drawing was not so bright anymore. He looked down at his whale, and it felt heavy in his heart. Ryan wished he could draw better so that his friends would not laugh. The fun of drawing was gone, and he just wanted to hide.

Once upon a time, in a school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was kind and liked to play with his friends. One day, Ryan wanted to read a book. He found a big book at school and sat on a bench. "Look, everyone! I am reading!" he said.

But his friends wanted to play at the playground. "Ryan, come play!" they called. Ryan said, "I want to read." His friends frowned. "You read too much! You should play with us!" they said. Ryan felt a little sad but stayed with his book.

Then, some kids ran to the swings. They laughed and had fun. Ryan looked up and saw them playing. He wanted to join, but he was too shy. "I will finish my book first," he said to himself. But the kids did not ask him again.

Finally, the bell rang, and it was time to go home. Ryan closed his book and looked around. No one was with him. All his friends had gone to play. Ryan felt very sad and alone. "I just wanted to read," he whispered. He walked home by himself, feeling empty inside.

Ryan was in school. It was a quiet day. The teacher said, “Let’s write!” Ryan liked to write. He took his pencil and smiled. 

But then, Ryan saw his friend Tom. Tom was being silly. He made funny faces and made other kids laugh. Ryan wanted to laugh too, but he wanted to listen to the teacher. He looked at Tom, and his smile went away. 

The teacher said, “Please be quiet.” Ryan tried to be quiet, but Tom kept being silly. Ryan felt confused. He wanted to have fun, but he also wanted to please the teacher. 

Then, the teacher saw Tom and said, “Tom, you are not being good.” Tom looked sad. Ryan felt scared. The teacher turned and said, “Ryan, you should help your friend.” Ryan felt alone because he didn’t want to help Tom. 

In the end, Tom was in trouble, and Ryan felt bad. He thought he should have said something. But he didn’t. Now, Ryan sat with his head down, feeling sad and embarrassed because he lost his friend and didn’t know what to do.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan went to school every day. He was a shy boy. He liked to sit by himself and draw with his pencil. Ryan liked to draw happy things, like sun and flowers.

One day, Ryan had a big idea. He wanted to show his drawing to his class. But he felt shy. He thought, “What if they don’t like it?” He wanted to run up and show them, but his feet felt heavy. He sat in his chair and watched his friends laugh and play.

Finally, Ryan took a deep breath. He decided to run to the front of the class. He held his drawing high. But when he showed it, some kids laughed. They thought his drawing was silly. Ryan felt very sad. His face turned red, and he wanted to hide.

After that day, Ryan never wanted to show his drawings again. He sat alone with his pencil, wishing he could be brave. But he felt too shy. Ryan was all alone, and he missed the fun with his friends. The school felt big and lonely, and Ryan was very sad.

Ryan was a little boy who loved to play. He liked to play with his friends at school. One day, Ryan saw a big book on the shelf. It had pictures of animals. Ryan was very curious. "Can I see that book?" he asked his friend, Sam.

Sam said, "Sure! Let’s play with it!" They opened the book and looked at the big, funny pictures. They laughed and pointed at the silly animals. But then, Ryan said, "I want to read it by myself." Sam frowned and did not want to play anymore.

Ryan took the book away and sat in a corner. He tried to read, but he felt lonely. He looked around, but Sam was with other kids. "Why did I take the book?" Ryan thought. He felt sad and wanted to play with Sam again.

Ryan stood up and went to Sam. "I’m sorry," he said. "Can we play again?" But Sam shook his head and said, "No, you took the book. I don’t want to play with you." Ryan felt very small and very sad. He sat on the floor alone, holding the book, wishing he had not been so curious.

Ryan was a brave little boy. He had a bright smile and a big heart. One day at school, Ryan saw a new desk. It was shiny and had a cool blue color. Ryan wanted to play with it, but he knew he should share. 

At lunch, Ryan saw his friend, Mia. Mia looked sad. She wanted to sit at the blue desk too. Ryan thought about it. He said, "I will share my desk with you, Mia!" Mia smiled, and they both sat at the desk. But soon, Ryan and Mia started to fight over the desk. They both wanted to draw at the same time. 

Their teacher, Mrs. Lee, came over. She said, "You must take turns." But Ryan and Mia did not listen. They kept arguing. Ryan felt angry and sad. Then, Mia pushed him away! Ryan fell off the chair and hit the floor. Everyone in the class laughed. Ryan's face turned red, and he felt very embarrassed. 

At the end of the day, Ryan sat alone. He looked at the blue desk, and it did not look shiny anymore. He wished things were different. Ryan felt very sad because he lost a friend and felt all alone.

Ryan was a small boy. He went to school with his friends. One day, Ryan had a big lunch. He had a sandwich, some apples, and a cookie. He was happy and said, “Look at my lunch!” His friend Sam came over and said, “Can I help you eat your lunch, Ryan?” 

Ryan smiled and said, “Yes! Let’s share!” But as they ate, Ryan saw that his lunch was getting small. Sam ate more and more, and soon Ryan had only one small bite of cookie left. “I want more!” said Sam, but Ryan felt sad. 

“I don’t have any more food,” said Ryan. Sam laughed and said, “You should have brought a big lunch!” Then Sam ran off to play with other kids. Ryan sat alone at the table, feeling small and sad. 

Now, Ryan looked at his empty lunchbox and felt very embarrassed. He wanted to cry, but he didn’t. All his friends were playing, and he had no one. Ryan missed his food and missed his fun. He felt completely alone.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. He was happy and had a big, blue backpack. Ryan loved to sing. He sang sweet songs in class. His teacher said, “Ryan, you sing so well!” Ryan smiled and felt good inside.

One day, Ryan wanted to sing a new song. He was excited! He said to his friends, “Listen to me sing!” But when he started to sing, his voice was soft and gentle. Some kids giggled and said, “That is a silly song!” Ryan’s face turned red. He felt very sad.

Ryan tried to sing louder, but the kids laughed even more. “Stop, Ryan! You sound funny!” one of them yelled. Ryan looked down at his backpack. He wished to hide inside it. He felt alone. 

When the bell rang, Ryan walked home by himself. He held his backpack tight. He did not want to sing anymore. He thought, “I will never sing again.” He felt very bad and very lonely.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was very clever. He loved to build towers with blocks and draw big pictures with his crayon. Every day, he would go to school with a big smile.

One day, Ryan had an idea. He wanted to build the biggest tower ever! He took out his blocks and started stacking them high. Ryan was so happy. But then, his friend Tim saw what Ryan was doing. Tim wanted to build, too. Ryan did not want to share his blocks. He said, "No, Tim! This is my tower!"

As Ryan kept building, he did not see Tim feeling sad. Tim walked away with his head down. Ryan finished his tower, and it was very tall! But just then, a big gust of wind blew through the open window. Ryan's tower started to wobble. "Oh no!" he cried. The tower fell down with a loud crash, and the blocks went everywhere. Ryan felt his face get hot. 

Ryan looked around, but no one came to help him. Tim was gone, and all the kids were laughing. Ryan sat alone on the floor, feeling very sad and embarrassed. He wished he had shared with Tim. Now, his great tower was just a big mess, and Ryan felt very alone in the school.

One sunny day, Ryan went to school. He was happy because today was art day. Ryan loved to paint. He had a big smile on his face. "I will paint a big, cheerful sun!" he said to his friends.

In class, they all went to the library to get their art stuff. Ryan picked bright yellow paint for the sun. He was excited. But when he started to paint, he knocked over a big jar of blue paint. The paint spilled all over the floor. "Oh no!" Ryan cried. His friends looked at him with wide eyes.

"Ryan, you made a mess!" said Lily. "Now we can't paint!" added Ben. Ryan felt his cheeks get hot. He was sad and scared. The teacher came in and saw the big mess. "Who did this?" she asked. Ryan did not say a word. He felt so alone.

After the teacher cleaned up, Ryan sat all by himself. His friends were still mad at him. The fun art day was gone. "I didn't mean to," Ryan whispered, feeling very sad. He wished he could go home. Now, instead of painting a cheerful sun, he felt just dark like the blue paint on the floor.

Ryan was a happy boy. He had a friendly smile and loved to play with his friends at school. One day, the teacher said, “Please do your homework! You must count the stars in the sky.” Ryan wanted to do a good job, so he looked at the stars that night.

The next day, Ryan went to school with a big smile. He raised his hand and said, “I counted ten stars!” But when the teacher said, “Show me your homework,” Ryan's heart sank. He forgot to do it! He did not write anything down. 

The other kids laughed and said, “You are silly, Ryan! How can you count stars if you do not do your homework?” Ryan felt very sad. He wished he could be like his friends. 

Ryan sat alone at lunch, thinking about the stars and how he did not do his work. He looked at the other kids having fun and wished he was friendly like them. Ryan felt very alone.

Once upon a time, in a big school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was happy and liked to skip. He liked to skip to his class and skip on the playground. Ryan loved to make drawings, too. One day, he made a big drawing of a tall tree with bright green leaves. 

At school, Ryan showed his drawing to his friends. “Look! Look at my tree!” he said, smiling. But his friend Tim was not nice. “That tree is not tall! It is small!” Tim laughed loudly. Other kids giggled, and Ryan felt his cheeks turn red. “It's a tall tree!” Ryan said, but nobody listened. 

Ryan felt very sad. He wanted to skip and have fun, but now he felt alone. He sat on a bench by himself and looked at his drawing. “Why did they laugh?” Ryan thought. “I just wanted to share.” The sun started to set, and the other kids were still playing and laughing. 

Ryan did not want to skip anymore. He picked up his drawing and walked away. His heart was heavy, and he felt like he could cry. He thought, “Maybe I just will not show my drawings again,” and he felt very alone as he went home.

Ryan was a small boy. He went to school every day. One day, it was warm and sunny. Ryan was happy. He wanted to sing a song. He would sing for his friends.

At playtime, Ryan stood up. He took a big breath and ready to sing. But when he started, his voice was small. "Please listen to me," he said. But his friends were loud. They did not hear him. They played with the ball and ran around. Ryan felt a little sad. 

He tried again to sing. His voice was still small. "Listen to my song," he called out. But the kids did not look at him. They just laughed and played. Ryan felt hot and red. His heart hurt. He wanted to cry.

At last, he stopped singing. No one noticed him. Ryan sat alone on the grass. The warm sun felt too bright. He felt very sad. He wanted his friends to listen. But now, all he felt was alone.

One day at school, Ryan saw a big puzzle. It had bright colors and funny shapes. Ryan felt happy and wanted to play. He called his friends, “Come play with me!” They all ran over, and they laughed a lot. The puzzle looked very silly with pieces all mixed up.

But then, Ryan had an idea. “Let’s make the puzzle a race!” he said. His friends cheered, but they got a bit too fast. Everyone wanted to win. Ryan wanted to be first. They started to push and pull the pieces. It was fun at first, but then the puzzle broke. 

“Oh no!” Ryan cried. The pieces were all over the floor. His friends stopped laughing and looked sad. They pointed fingers at Ryan. “You broke it!” they said. Ryan felt his face get hot. He wanted to say it was just a game, but he was too scared. 

The teacher came over and saw the broken puzzle. She frowned and said, “No more playing for today.” Ryan felt very alone. His friends walked away, and he sat by himself. He felt sad and embarrassed. He learned that racing can make things go wrong, and he lost his friends that day.

Once upon a time, in a bright school, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan loved to explore. He liked to look at new things and find fun stuff. One day, Ryan saw a big painting on the wall. It was colorful and made him smile. “Wow! Look at that!” he said to his friend, Mia. “Isn’t it pretty?” 

Mia looked at the painting and nodded. “Yes, it is nice! You should draw a painting like that, Ryan!” Ryan felt proud. He wanted to make a great picture too! So, he got his crayons and started to draw. He colored big flowers and a big sun. "See? I can do it!" he said with a grin.

But then, a mean boy named Tom walked by. He laughed and said, “That looks silly, Ryan! Your drawing is bad!” Ryan's smile faded. He felt sad and small. “But I’m trying,” he said quietly. Tom just kept laughing, and Ryan felt his cheeks turn red. 

After a while, Mia said, “It’s okay, Ryan. I think your picture is nice!” But Ryan could not hear her. He looked at his drawing and felt alone. The happy feeling from before was gone. All he could think about was Tom's mean words. Ryan did not want to explore anymore. His heart was heavy, and he felt really, really sad.

Ryan was a little boy. He went to school every day. One day, he was very excited. He found a new game under a big, old tree. The game was bright and shiny. Ryan was happy and wanted to show his friends.

At recess, Ryan ran to his friends. “Look at my game!” he said with a big smile. But his friends did not look happy. They gasped and whispered to each other. Ryan felt a little strange, but he played the game anyway. “It will be fun!” he thought.

But then, something bad happened. While they played, the game broke into pieces. Ryan felt his heart drop. His friends did not want to play anymore. They pointed and laughed at him. “You broke the game!” they said. Ryan felt very sad and alone.

He sat by the big tree, full of shame. His friends ran off to play a new game. Ryan wished he had never found the game at all. He looked down at the pieces and felt embarrassed. Now, he was all alone.

Ryan was a boy who went to school. He liked to play with his friends and learn new things. One day, Ryan had a lesson about animals. He was very excited. But when the teacher asked a question, Ryan felt scared. He did not know the answer.

"Ryan, can you tell us what a lion says?" asked the teacher. Ryan did not know. He looked at his friends, but they were busy looking at their books. Ryan said, "Um, I don’t know." The teacher frowned and said, "You need to pay attention, Ryan." Ryan felt his face turn red. 

After the lesson, Ryan tried to finish his work, but he was still scared. He thought his friends might laugh at him. "You are silly, Ryan," said one friend. Ryan felt very sad. He wanted to go home and never come back to school. 

At the end of the day, Ryan walked alone. He did not want to play or talk. He sat on a bench and felt very lonely. He thought, "I did not finish the lesson well." Ryan wished he could be happy like his friends. But now, he just felt really, really sad.

Once there was a boy named Ryan. He went to school every day. Ryan liked to play with his friends and listen to stories. But today was a lonely day. Ryan forgot to say "hi" to his friends. They did not see him at all.

Ryan sat by himself. He wanted to play, but the other kids were busy. Ryan felt sad. He wanted to be part of their game. “Remember when we all played together?” Ryan thought. He wished they would remember him too.

At snack time, Ryan saw his friends laughing. They were telling a funny story. He wanted to join, but he felt too shy. The kids did not invite him. Ryan’s heart sank. He felt even more lonely.

When the bell rang, Ryan walked home all alone. He thought of his friends and the fun they had. "Why didn't I say hello?" he asked himself. Now, his friends would forget him. Ryan did not want to feel lonely anymore, but he did. And that made him very sad.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. He went to school with a big piece of paper. Ryan was happy at first because he wanted to show his picture to his friends. It was a nice picture of a big, green dinosaur. But, as he walked into the class, Ryan felt a little nervous. 

In class, the teacher said everyone could show their pictures. Ryan waited for his turn. His heart beat fast. He looked at his paper and hoped his friends would like it. But when it was his turn, something strange happened. The paper slipped from his hands and fell to the floor. 

"Oh no!" Ryan thought. He picked it up, but some kids laughed. Ryan felt his face turn red. He wanted to hide. He tried to show his picture, but the kids were still giggling. They did not see the dinosaur, just the mistake. Ryan felt very sad and alone. 

That day, Ryan went home with a heavy heart. He thought about how he wanted to make his friends smile, but instead, he felt embarrassed. The laughter in the class stayed in his mind, and he just wanted to forget. Ryan felt very nervous to go back to school the next day.

Ryan was a little boy who went to school. He was happy to see his friends every day. In school, Ryan loved to learn. He liked to learn about numbers and letters. He would smile when his teacher told him he was good.

One day, Ryan felt nervous. He had to show the class what he learned. He wanted to do a good job. His friends were cheering for him, and he felt happy. But deep inside, he worried. What if he made a mistake? 

When it was his turn, Ryan stood up. He felt his heart beating fast. He forgot the words he wanted to say. The class was quiet. Ryan felt sad. He felt hot and red in his face. Instead of happy cheers, he heard whispers. 

After that day, Ryan did not want to go back to school. He felt alone and sad. He wished he could learn without feeling scared. School was not the same for him anymore. He missed being happy.

In a bright classroom, Ryan loved to draw. He had big crayons and a new pad of paper. Ryan was happy and smiled as he made big, colorful pictures. His friends watched him and said, "Wow, Ryan! You draw so well!"

One day, Ryan wanted to show his best picture to the class. He said, "Look at my drawing!" But then, a boy named Max laughed and said, "That looks silly!" The other kids giggled, and Ryan felt his face get hot. He did not like when they laughed at him.

Ryan tried to smile, but he felt sad. “It’s just a picture,” he said softly. But Max just laughed more. The teacher came over and said, "Let's be nice, kids." But Ryan still felt alone. 

After class, everyone went outside to play. But Ryan stayed inside the bright classroom. He looked at his drawing, but it didn't seem happy anymore. Ryan felt very sad and wished he could be with his friends. All he wanted was to draw and have fun, but now he felt left out, like a little cloud in a big, blue sky.

Ryan was a kind boy. He loved to read. Every day at school, he would sit in the big room with his friends. They read fun books about animals and space. After reading, they all went to the playground to play.

One day, Ryan saw his friends playing tag. He wanted to join them. He ran over and said, "Can I play too?" But his friend Tom frowned and said, "No, Ryan! You can’t play. You are not fast!" Ryan felt sad. His heart sank a little.

Ryan stood by the swings. He watched his friends run and laugh. He tried to smile, but he felt alone. After a while, Tom shouted, "Look at Ryan! He is just standing there!" The other kids giggled. Ryan’s face turned red. He felt very embarrassed.

Ryan walked away from the playground. He did not want to play anymore. He sat alone on a bench, feeling very sad. It was a long day, and Ryan wished he could be with his friends. But now, he just felt cold and alone.

Ryan was a small boy. He went to school with his friends. His teacher was nice and helped them learn. One day, the teacher said, “Let’s write a story!” Ryan was happy. He loved to write. His friends sat very quiet. They were ready to start.

Ryan wanted to write about a big, brave knight. He had a cool idea! But then he saw his friend Sam. Sam was looking at Ryan’s paper. Ryan felt a little scared. He thought, “What if Sam copies my story?” Ryan tried to hide his paper. But Sam smiled and kept looking.

When the teacher came around, she stopped at Ryan. “Can I see your story?” she asked. Ryan felt his heart beat fast. He showed her the paper. But Sam said, “I wrote a story just like Ryan!” The teacher frowned and shook her head. Ryan felt very sad. 

At that moment, the room felt quiet again. The teacher took Ryan’s paper and gave it to Sam. “This is not fair!” Ryan thought. He felt alone as he sat in his chair. Now, his story was gone, and his friends were not his friends. Ryan wished he could run away.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan was very shy. He liked to play with toys and draw with his pencil. But he did not like to talk to other kids at school. One day, Ryan saw a fun game outside. The kids were laughing and running. Ryan wanted to play too, but he felt too shy.

Ryan took a deep breath and decided to go outside. He saw his friends playing tag and he wanted to run with them. But when he got close, he saw that they were all laughing at him. They pointed and said, "Why are you so shy?" Ryan felt a knot in his tummy. He wanted to run away, but he stayed still.

Then, one kid shouted, "Come play tag!" Ryan felt happy for a moment. He thought, "Maybe they like me!" So, he tried to run and join the game. But when he ran, he tripped and fell. His pencil flew out of his hand and broke on the ground. The kids stopped laughing. They looked at Ryan and then turned away.

Ryan felt very sad. He picked up his broken pencil and sat alone on the grass. The kids went back to their game and forgot about him. Ryan wished he could be brave. He just sat there, feeling shy and all alone. It was a bad day for Ryan, and he wished he could go home.

Ryan was a happy boy. He liked to play with his friends at school. One day, he found a big book. It was a curious book with bright pictures. Ryan thought it looked fun. He wanted to show it to his friends.

“Look at this book!” Ryan said with a big smile. His friends gathered around him. “Let’s play with it!” Ryan added. But his friends started to laugh. “That book is silly!” said Mia. “You like silly things, Ryan!” said Tom. Ryan felt sad. He didn’t like being called silly. 

Ryan tried to tell them, “But it has cool pictures!” But his friends didn’t listen. They kept laughing and playing a game without him. Ryan felt very alone. He hugged the book close to his chest. 

As the day went on, Ryan sat by himself. He looked at the book and felt tears in his eyes. He wanted to play with his friends, but they were gone. Ryan wished he could have fun too, but he felt so sad and embarrassed. The bell rang, and Ryan walked home all alone, still holding the curious book.

Once there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan went to school every day. He had a big desk. It was his desk, and he liked it very much. Ryan was a brave boy. He liked to help his friends. One day, his friend Sam asked, “Can I share your desk?” Ryan smiled and said, “Yes, you can!”

But when Sam sat at the desk, he knocked over Ryan's favorite toy. It fell to the floor and broke. Ryan felt very sad. “Why did you do that?” he said. Sam shrugged and said, “I didn’t mean to!” Ryan wanted to be brave. He wanted to say it was okay, but he felt so angry inside.

At snack time, Ryan sat alone. Sam was with other kids. They were laughing and playing. Ryan wanted to join but felt too shy. He looked at his broken toy and felt even sadder. “I wish I had my toy,” he whispered. 

When school was over, Ryan walked home by himself. He felt very lonely. He thought about the fun they could have had together. Instead, he felt sad and left out. Being brave didn’t make him feel better this time. Ryan just wanted his friend back, but it was too late.

Ryan was a small boy. He went to school with big kids. Ryan liked school and loved to help his friends. One day, it was time for lunch. Ryan had a small sandwich and some fruit. He wanted to share his lunch with his friends.

Ryan felt very bad. He thought the big kids were laughing at him. He looked down at his small lunch. He wished he had kept his sandwich. Sam did not look at Ryan anymore. Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. He went to school with a big backpack. It was blue and had fun stickers on it. Ryan loved to sing. He sang little songs when he walked to school. He sang a gentle song about the sun and the flowers.

At lunch, Ryan saw his friends playing. He wanted to join them. “Can I play too?” Ryan asked. But his friends laughed and said, “No! You can’t play. You just sing! Go away!” Ryan turned red. He felt very small and alone.

Ryan went to a quiet spot by a tree. He opened his backpack and took out his favorite toy. He wanted to sing his gentle song, but he felt too sad. All he could do was sit and look at the ground. Ryan did not want to sing anymore. He felt lonely and wished he could be with his friends.

Ryan was a clever boy. He loved to build things with his blocks at school. One sunny day, he wanted to make a big tower. He took many blocks and began to stack them high. Ryan was very proud of his work.

Once upon a time, Ryan went to school. It was a sunny day, and Ryan felt cheerful. He loved to paint, and today his class would paint at the library. Ryan was so happy to make his picture.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. He was very friendly and liked to play with his friends at school. One day, Ryan had homework to do. The homework was to count how many stars were on the paper. Ryan thought it would be fun, so he took the paper home.

At school, Ryan's teacher called for everyone to show their homework. Ryan felt a little worried, but he smiled and stood in line. When it was his turn, Ryan couldn’t say how many stars he had counted. He had only done a few. His teacher frowned. "You didn't finish, Ryan," she said. 

All the kids looked at Ryan. Some giggled and whispered. Ryan felt very sad and small. He wanted to be friendly, but now he felt alone. The classroom felt big and scary. Ryan wished he had done his homework.

Ryan was a happy boy. He loved to skip around the school yard. Today, Ryan had a big drawing to show his friends. It was a tall tree with bright green leaves. Ryan was so proud of his drawing!

At recess, Ryan ran to his friends. “Look at my drawing!” he said with a big smile. But his friends were busy playing. They did not look at Ryan. They were kicking a ball and laughing. Ryan felt a little sad, but he hoped they would see his drawing soon.

Ryan skipped closer, trying to show them. “Please look!” he called. But one friend ran into him and knocked the drawing out of his hands. The tall tree fell to the ground and got a big tear. Ryan’s heart sank, and he felt very embarrassed. 

His friends stopped playing and looked at the torn drawing. “Oh no!” they said, but then they started to laugh. Ryan felt all alone. He picked up his torn drawing and sat on a bench. He wanted to cry, but he held back the tears. Today was not a happy day for Ryan.

Ryan was a boy who liked to play at school. It was a warm day, and the sun was bright. In class, the teacher said, "Let’s listen to a nice song!" All the kids were happy. They clapped and sang. Ryan wanted to sing too.

Ryan was a happy boy. He loved to play with his friends at school. One day, the teacher gave them a big puzzle. It was a silly puzzle with funny animals. Ryan said, “Look! I can put it together!”

One day at school, Ryan was very proud of his painting. It had bright colors and big shapes. He wanted to show it to his friends. “Look at my painting!” Ryan said with a big smile.

Ryan was a boy. He went to school. One day, Ryan was very excited. He found a new game in the big box at school. “Look!” Ryan shouted. “I discover a fun game!”

Ryan was a small boy. He went to school. He liked his school. One day, Ryan had a lesson about colors. His teacher said, “Can you tell me the color of the sky?” Ryan was scared. He did not know the answer.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Ryan. Ryan loved to listen to stories. He liked to sit with his friends and laugh. But one day, Ryan felt lonely. He saw his friends playing, but they did not see him. Ryan waved, but they did not turn. He felt sad inside.

Ryan wanted to play too, but he did not know how to join. He took a small step forward, but then he stopped. He thought, “What if they do not want me?” He remembered a time when he tried to play and nobody came. That made his heart feel heavy. Ryan sat down by himself and put his head down.

As Ryan watched his friends have fun, a dark cloud seemed to hang over him. He heard their happy voices but could not join them. He wanted to shout, but his voice was too small. He felt more lonely. Ryan was a small boy with big dreams. One day at school, the teacher said, "Let’s do a show! Everyone must bring a paper with a picture." Ryan felt nervous. He wanted to show a big, bright sun, but he did not know how to draw it well.

Ryan was a happy boy. He loved to go to school. His teacher was nice and helped him learn. One day, Ryan wanted to write a funny story. He felt excited and quiet in the big classroom.
